title: Taking Risks in Business
subtitle: Lessons from the Racing Track
excerpt: Ayrton Senna once said: if you no longer go for a gap that exists, you are no longer a racing driver. The same is true in business. But knowing when to go — and when the gap is an illusion — is what separates great leaders from reckless ones.

title: Hindsight Bias
subtitle: The Silent Killer of Institutional Learning
excerpt: The 'I knew it all along' reflex feels harmless. It isn't. When outcomes rewrite the past, organizations stop diagnosing mistakes properly — and everyone absolves themselves. Revisiting Kahneman reminded me how deeply corrosive this is for teams.

title: Some Races Never End
subtitle: Only the Baton Changes Hands
excerpt: In 1967, an official tried to physically remove Kathrine Switzer from the Boston Marathon mid-race. She finished. Then she ran until women were officially accepted in 1972. Today my daughter Alara pursues her doctorate at University of Toronto. She didn't need to ask permission to run.
